body.text: This paper examines sensory integration challenges in neurodivergent individuals and how cognitive prosthetics can support multisensory processing to reduce overload and improve perceptual coherence.

Many neurodivergent individuals experience atypical sensory processing, including hypersensitivity, hyposensitivity, and sensory seeking behaviors. Cognitive prosthetics must address these differences to be effective.
Sensory processing disorder prevalence in neurodivergent populations
Impact on daily functioning and quality of life
Need for customizable sensory profiles in assistive technology
Node Zero hardware and The Buffer software incorporate sensory integration principles through customizable feedback, adjustable sensitivity, and multimodal interaction options.
